notes: SHIP v1.14.332 - Give the INSPECT-3D Command hero its own control strip (VIEW RAIL + EXPLODE + CABLES). BUG (owner-reported): in rackElevation_render3D the CABLES chip and the VIEW-RAIL/EXPLODE row only built when _strip = the reh3d PANEL toolbar (.reh-3d-toggle) existed. The Command hero mount (#cc-rackhero) has no panel chrome, so both gates were false and the hero rendered a bare scene with no controls. FIX (owner-directed, touches rackElevation_render3D interior): resolve the control-strip HOST from the mount's OWN ancestry first - if mountEl.closest('.cc-rackhero') (the hero), build/own a hero-scoped strip div (class 'reh-3d-toggle reh-3d-toggle--hero') inside that card and point _strip at it; only a non-hero mount (the panel) falls back to document.querySelector('.reh-3d-toggle'). The SAME CABLES + rail/EXPLODE code path then runs for both hosts (ONE code path, two hosts; rail logic NOT duplicated). ⚠RD-REVIEW CATCH (fixed pre-commit): the first draft resolved the hero via a bare document.querySelector('.reh-3d-toggle') and only fell back when it was null - but a rack-detail 3D view opened under Work leaves a HIDDEN .reh-3d-toggle in #pg-work (pages hide, never delete), which the global query mistook for 'the panel exists' → it skipped the hero fallback and silently built the hero controls into the hidden Work page = bare hero, no signal (a No-Silent-Failures violation reproducing the very bug this ship fixes). Corrected to resolve hero-first from mountEl's own card, so a stale node in another hidden page can never be matched. Same scoping applied to the rail existence check: _strip.parentNode.querySelector('#reh3dRail') instead of the global document.getElementById('reh3dRail'). Teardown is symmetric: _heroStrip removed alongside _cablesBtn/_railEl (panel's .reh-3d-toggle pre-exists and is left alone; _heroStrip is null on the panel path = no-op there). New var _heroStrip declared WITH _railEl/_explodeBtn at the top (declare-above-first-use). CSS: hero controls are 2 stacked centered rows at the top of the card (row1 CABLES; row2 FRONT/ISO/TOP/REAR/EXPLODE) so they never share one no-wrap row at 390px; flex-wrap zero-overflow backstop; scoped #pg-cmd .cc-rackhero (ID specificity beats the shared body.rd .reh-3d-rail base); reuses .reh-3d-seg styling so the hero controls look identical to the panel's. RACK SCENE LOCK: touches ONLY control-chrome DOM + its CSS + teardown - NO scene materials/lights/fog/tone-mapping/tray-geometry/camera/floor/reflection (P0-verified by phantom-rd-reviewer). ?legacy byte-identical: fallback gated to .cc-rackhero (redesign-only); panel/legacy path keeps _strip = .reh-3d-toggle, _heroStrip null. ADVISORY (pre-existing, tracked, NOT introduced here): .reh-3d-seg buttons ~22px < the 44px gloved floor - already a backlog item; this ship doubles their on-screen count, worth an owner ruling later. Gates: inline-JS vm.Script compile x3, sw.js ok, version.json valid, CSS brace net-0, three-stamp .331->.332 lockstep; phantom-ship-gate + phantom-rd-reviewer both PASS pre-commit (rd-reviewer CHANGES-REQUESTED on the first draft, re-run after the host-resolution fix). DEVICE-VERIFY (installed PWA, hard-refresh first): load a Master -> Command hero shows the control rows (FRONT/ISO/TOP/REAR + EXPLODE + CABLES) overlaid at the top; view presets re-aim, EXPLODE extends trays, CABLES toggles runs (or honest disabled state); CRITICAL ORDER CHECK: open a rack's 3D view under Work, then bottom-nav back to Command -> hero STILL shows its controls (not bare); leave+return re-mounts, no stranded/doubled strip; panel rack view UNCHANGED; 390px = 2 rows. ROLLBACK: revert this commit (hero renders bare as .330/.331); no asset/data change.
